title: Creative Event Reason Code
description: Categorical reason carried on creative lifecycle webhooks (`creative.status_changed`, `creative.purged`). Distinct from `impairment-reason-code` because creative-policy reasons differ in vocabulary and remediation paths from media-buy delivery impairments ‚Äî a creative may be revoked for a content-policy reason that produces no impairment on any buy, and an impairment may close without any creative-side status change. Buyers correlate the two via `creative_id` when both surfaces fire.

enumDescriptions.review_passed: Initial content-policy review succeeded ‚Äî used on `pending_review ‚Üí approved`. No buyer action required. The default reason for a clean first approval and the value sellers MUST emit when no more specific reason applies.
enumDescriptions.review_failure: Initial human or automated content-policy review failed ‚Äî used on `pending_review ‚Üí rejected`. Distinct from `policy_revocation` because no prior approval existed. Buyer remediation: address the policy concern and resubmit via `sync_creatives`.
enumDescriptions.processing_failure: Seller's automated processing pipeline failed ‚Äî corrupt file, unsupported codec, asset reference unreachable, transcoding error. Used on `processing ‚Üí rejected`. Buyer remediation: fix the underlying asset and resubmit via `sync_creatives`.
enumDescriptions.seller_rereview: Seller pulled an approved creative back to pending review without an immediate rejection ‚Äî typically a policy update, a flagged-content investigation, or a sampling-driven re-review. Used on `approved ‚Üí pending_review`. Buyer remediation: wait for the re-review outcome; no buyer action available.
enumDescriptions.policy_revocation: Seller content-policy decision applied after initial approval ‚Äî the seller's policy changed or a previously-overlooked violation was caught. Used on `approved ‚Üí rejected`. When the buyer's asset/destination changed (rather than the seller's policy), use `content_drift` instead. Buyer remediation: review against the seller's updated policy and resubmit a compliant variant via `sync_creatives`.
enumDescriptions.content_drift: Landing page or referenced content changed after approval in a way that breaks the original review (e.g., destination URL now serves different content, asset host swapped). Used on `approved ‚Üí pending_review` (re-review pulled) or `approved ‚Üí rejected` (revocation). When in doubt between `content_drift` and `policy_revocation`, prefer `content_drift` when buyer remediation involves restoring the original asset/destination; prefer `policy_revocation` when the seller's policy itself changed. Buyer remediation: restore the original content or resubmit with the new destination for fresh review.
enumDescriptions.identity_authorization_revoked: Authorization for an identity or post reference was revoked after approval. Used on `approved ‚Üí suspended` for recoverable published-post reference failures, or `suspended ‚Üí rejected` when the seller determines the authorization cannot be restored. Buyer remediation: restore authorization through the platform or replace the referenced post.
enumDescriptions.identity_authorization_expired: Authorization for an identity or post reference expired after approval. Used on `approved ‚Üí suspended` for recoverable published-post reference failures. Buyer remediation: renew authorization through the platform.
enumDescriptions.source_private: The referenced source post became private or otherwise inaccessible to the seller after approval. Used on `approved ‚Üí suspended` while the buyer or creator can restore visibility. Buyer remediation: restore source visibility or replace the referenced post.
enumDescriptions.source_deleted: The referenced source post was deleted or became permanently unavailable. Used on `approved ‚Üí archived`, `approved ‚Üí rejected`, or `creative.purged` depending on the seller's retention and active-assignment constraints. Buyer remediation: replace the referenced post; the deleted source itself cannot be recovered through AdCP.
enumDescriptions.takedown_request: Third-party legal or IP takedown notice forced the seller to disable the creative ‚Äî DMCA, trademark, defamation, court order, or equivalent. Used on `approved ‚Üí rejected` or destruction (`creative.purged`, either purge_kind). Buyer remediation: dispute the takedown out-of-band with the seller; protocol-side remediation is unavailable.
enumDescriptions.advertiser_request: Buyer-initiated takedown that propagated through the seller's review queue (the advertiser's legal or trust-and-safety team asked the seller to pull a creative the buyer had already approved internally). Distinct from `takedown_request` (third-party request) ‚Äî `advertiser_request` originates with the buying side and is voluntary. Used on `approved ‚Üí rejected` or `creative.purged`. No buyer-side remediation needed; the buyer initiated the action.
enumDescriptions.seller_archive: Seller-initiated archive ‚Äî covers inactivity, post-flight expiry, storage-tier policy, and other operational seller-side cleanup. Used on `approved ‚Üí archived`. Buyer remediation: unarchive via `sync_creatives` if the creative is needed again; no content change required.
enumDescriptions.account_closed: Account-level cascade: the owning account transitioned to `closed`, and the seller is purging or archiving the account's creatives as part of teardown. Used on `creative.purged` (typically `purge_kind: soft` first; `hard` if legal disposition requires). Buyer remediation: none on the protocol side ‚Äî the account is gone.
enumDescriptions.account_suspended: Account-level cascade: the owning account transitioned to `suspended`, and the seller is archiving the account's creatives until the account is reactivated. Used on `approved ‚Üí archived`. Buyer remediation: resolve the account suspension out-of-band; archived creatives can be unarchived once the account is active again.
enumDescriptions.retention_expired: Creative reached the end of its retention window and was purged. Used on `creative.purged` with `purge_kind: soft` only ‚Äî retention-driven purges retain a tombstone for the 30-day webhook activity retention window. Buyer remediation: re-upload via `sync_creatives` if the creative is still needed.
enumDescriptions.legal_erasure: Creative purged under a legal erasure obligation (GDPR Article 17, CCPA deletion, equivalent). Used on `creative.purged` with `purge_kind: hard` only ‚Äî no tombstone is retained and the seller MUST NOT echo the asset content elsewhere. Buyer remediation: none on the seller side; the buyer's own systems may also need to honor the erasure.
